content.text:
All 33 tests pass. I added `test/merge.test.js` with 32 tests covering every documented guarantee:

- **Empty/trivial** — `[] -> []`, single interval, degenerate `[n,n]` points
- **Overlap merging** — the documented `[1,4],[3,6] -> [1,6]`, containment, the "don't shrink the running end" case (`[1,10],[2,3]`), chained runs, duplicates, shared start points
- **Touching merging** — the documented `[1,2],[2,3] -> [1,3]`, edge-touching point intervals
- **Disjoint** — separated intervals stay apart, including the subtle single-unit gap (`[1,2],[3,4]` don't touch)
- **Sort order** — reversed and shuffled inputs come out sorted by start ascending
- **Numeric ranges** — negatives, zero-crossing, and fractional bounds
- **No mutation** — outer array length/order preserved, pair arrays untouched, results are fresh (non-aliased) arrays callers can safely mutate
- **RangeError** — inverted intervals (alone and mixed in), `start===end` is valid, error message names the offending pair, and rejection happens before any output is produced

I left `src/merge.js`, `package.json`, and the starter `example.test.js` untouched as instructed.

- **Empty/trivial** — `[] -> []`, single interval, degenerate `[n,n]` points
- **Overlap merging** — the documented `[1,4],[3,6] -> [1,6]`, containment, the "don't shrink the running end" case (`[1,10],[2,3]`), chained runs, duplicates, shared start points
- **Touching merging** — the documented `[1,2],[2,3] -> [1,3]`, edge-touching point intervals
- **Disjoint** — separated intervals stay apart, including the subtle single-unit gap (`[1,2],[3,4]` don't touch)
- **Sort order** — reversed and shuffled inputs come out sorted by start ascending
- **Numeric ranges** — negatives, zero-crossing, and fractional bounds
- **No mutation** — outer array length/order preserved, pair arrays untouched, results are fresh (non-aliased) arrays callers can safely mutate
- **RangeError** — inverted intervals (alone and mixed in), `start===end` is valid, error message names the offending pair, and rejection happens before any output is produced

I left `src/merge.js`, `package.json`, and the starter `example.test.js` untouched as instructed.
